title: Tamil Nadu CM Stalin convenes urgent virtual meeting of DMK MPs, district secretaries over delimitation issue

title: How delimitation based on 2011 Census would redraw India’s political map

content: The self-financing college teachers and students are also not part of the statutes of the respective universities, claim functionaries of Self-Financing College Teachers’ and Staff Association

content: The phone conversation between the two foreign ministers came amid reports of efforts by the U.S. and Iran to hold a fresh round of negotiations after the collapse of the initial dialogue in Islamabad

content: A citizen from Bihar voting in their State and voting in Maharashtra or Kerala will have different implications

title: Tamil Nadu Elections: Where even the powerful can lose
content: In Tamil Nadu, even the most powerful leaders have faced defeat at the ballot box. From Chief Ministers to national icons, voters have repeatedly asserted their authority. This video looks at key electoral moments that prove one truth of Tamil Nadu politics: no leader is bigger than the voter.

title: Chief Ministers of Tamil Nadu who were defeated in elections
content: From the early years of Dravidian politics to more recent decades, voters have not hesitated to defeat stalwarts, even Chief Ministers

content: YSRCP alleged its media wing general secretary Pudi Srihari was detained near Jagan Mohan Reddy’s Tadepalli residence over social media posts targeting the Chief Minister

content: Rajya Sabha member Anil Bonde, who hails from Amravati, demanded a probe into the matter by a Special Investigation Team

title: PM Modi a ‘misleader’, engaged in ‘deliberate deceit’ over delimitation: Congress
content: Congress general secretary in-charge communications Jairam Ramesh said that the prime minister is a so-called leader whose only distinguishing feature is his unmatched ability to be a ‘misleader’

title: Constitution Amendment Bill proposes possible change in size of State Assemblies
content: The proposed amendments, in effect, mean that State Assemblies will see a change in their total number of seats, depending on population changes and constituency boundaries within States will also be redrawn as part of the delimitation exercise

title: FIFA wishes Malayalees on Vishu, posts AI-generated picture of Messi alongside traditional Vishu Kani
content: The FIFA World Cup page also brightened the propitious day for Keralites by conveying the international sports body’s Vishu Greetings in Malayalam, “Vishu Ashamsakal”.

content: Upon investigation, it was found that the women had overslept in the ladies compartment even after the train had reached their destination

content: As Nitish Kumar is likely to resign this week, the Deputy Chief Minister is the frontrunner to be the BJP’s first Chief Minister of the State

title: Cat ‘custody’ row between neighbours ends amicably after Hoysala police intervention in Bengaluru
content: Tensions escalated when the female cat’s owners brought the kittens to their neighbour’s house and demanded that the latter assume responsibility for raising them, claiming their pet was the ‘father’

title: PM arrives at HAL airport in Bengaluru
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is heading to the Adichunchanagiri Mutt in Nagamangala taluk of Mandya district

title: Drunk driver rams car into group of people in Chikkamagaluru; 7-year-old girl dead, seven others injured
content: Chikkamagaluru Superintendent of Police Jitendra Kumar Dayama told The Hindu that the driver of the car hit a tractor before ploughing into a group of 11 persons

title: Delimitation cannot be an excuse for domination: Siddaramaiah
content: Karnataka Chief Minister says delimitation must not become a tool that reshapes representation to suit political convenience, or one that is pushed through by tying it to unrelated reforms

title: To be on electoral roll, to vote is a sentimental right, says Supreme Court
content: The act of voting is the biggest expression of nationality and patriotism, the EC departed from promise to leave voters in 2002 electoral roll untouched and the logical discrepancy category was unique to West Bengal, says apex court

title: Marathi made mandatory for auto, taxi drivers in Maharashtra from May 1
content: The rule will come into effect on Maharashtra Day, officials said on Tuesday (April 14, 2026). Transport Minister Pratap Sarnaik said a campaign will be carried out through the Motor Transport Department’s 59 offices across the State to check drivers

title: TCS Nashik scandal: FIRs map 4-yr pattern of sex abuse, coercion & religious targeting
content: NASHIK TCS News: A major investigation into a Nashik TCS unit reveals disturbing allegations of sexual exploitation, workplace harassment, and religious coercion spanning nearly four years. Employees reported inappropriate behavior, suggestive remarks, and unwanted advances. Some also faced religious targeting, including derogatory comments and pressure to convert. TCS has suspended accused employees and launched an internal probe.

title: 'Gambhir country ke liye kitno se dushmani le raha hai': Munaf Patel backs India coach
content: “Just remember this, if a head coach like Gautam Gambhir is removed, then handling the players will become very difficult. He is a genuine person; he calls the truth as it is and many people don’t like that," Munaf said while speaking on TOI Sports' Bombay Sport Exchange Podcast.

title: Bulldozer action in Amravati: House of Ayan Ahmed Tanveer demolished after abuse videos surface
content: Authorities in Amravati have launched a crackdown, including bulldozer action, against the residence of a 19-year-old accused in a shocking cyber-enabled sexual exploitation and blackmail case involving minors. The accused allegedly lured girls via social media, recorded objectionable content without consent, and circulated it online. Police have arrested the suspect and are investigating a wider network.

title: India-US trade deal back in focus: Indian delegation to visit Washington next week
content: The agreement had originally been expected to be signed in March, but developments in the Donald Trump tariff regime following a ruling by the Supreme Court of the United States have changed the circumstances.

title: Is Mayank Yadav ready for IPL? LSG share big update on Rs 11 crore speedster
content: Lucknow Super Giants are set to take on Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in what promises to be a high-octane IPL clash, with a potential major boost in the form of pace sensation Mayank Yadav’s return.

title: Ecstasy decoded: How a ‘party pill’ turned fatal at Mumbai concert
content: Two MBA students' deaths at a Mumbai concert highlight ecstasy's dangers. Known as Molly or E, this party drug, often in colourful pills, can cause fatal overheating and organ strain. Unregulated potency and adulterants, especially when combined with dancing and alcohol, amplify risks, making timely recognition of symptoms crucial.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ box office: Ranveer Singh’s film heads to beat ‘Pushpa 2’ in week 4
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its impressive box office run, now in its fourth week. The film has secured the third-highest week 4 collection in India, surpassing 'Chaava' and is on track to challenge 'Pushpa 2's' record. Its global earnings have reached over Rs 1,727 crore.

title: 400-tonne mobile crane topples at Mumbai Metro construction site; traffic hit
content: A major incident unfolded at a Mumbai metro construction site in BKC as a crane toppled, causing a heavy girder to collapse. The mishap occurred during the installation of a bridge beam, leading to injuries among workers and significant traffic disruption. Authorities have launched an inquiry into the mechanical failure, with operations halted.

title: Missed filing ITR on time? Why Delhi HC refused to condone delay in this case
content: The Delhi High Court examined the impugned order and the reasons furnished by the petitioner and found no merit in the challenge. The Court specifically rejected the submissions that the petitioner’s lack of awareness of tax laws or his alleged illiteracy could justify the delay. It held that such a plea was not tenable in law.

title: Aatmanirbhar Bharat 2.0 push: India working on self-reliance in energy, nuclear power
content: A key pillar of what is being described as Atmanirbhar 2.0 involves expanding capacity in green energy, nuclear power and thermal generation to lessen dependence on imported oil and gas.

title: Bihar's first BJP CM: Samrat Choudhary takes oath
content: Samrat Choudhary has been sworn in as Bihar's new Chief Minister, marking the BJP's first leader to hold the state's top post. This transition follows Nitish Kumar's departure after over two decades of leadership. Choudhary, a prominent OBC face and former deputy CM, previously served in various parties before joining the BJP in 2017.

title: AP inter results 2026: Pass percentage rises from 59% to 81% in 6 years
content: AP Inter 2026 results show continued improvement in pass percentages for both first and second year students in Andhra Pradesh. First year recorded 77% and second year 81%, with over four lakh candidates in each category. Over six years, results indicate steady growth across general and vocational streams. Government Junior Colleges performed lower than overall averages. Krishna district led performance while Annamayya was lowest. Recounting, re-verification and supplementary exams were also announced.

title: Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’ piracy case escalates as film gets aired on cable TV; cable operator arrested
content: Thalapathy Vijay's film 'Jana Nayagan' faces a major piracy crisis. After an online leak, the movie was illegally aired on a local cable TV channel in Coimbatore. A cable operator has been arrested and equipment seized. This incident adds to the film's troubles, which already faced release delays due to certification issues.

title: 'Punjab poll prep has begun': Kejriwal on ED action against AAP MP who replaced Raghav Chadha
content: Aam Aadmi Party leader Arvind Kejriwal and Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann have accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of using the Enforcement Directorate's raid on AAP Rajya Sabha MP Ashok Mittal as election preparation for Punjab. The ED action occurred shortly after Mittal was appointed deputy leader in the Rajya Sabha, replacing Raghav Chadha.

title: Investors gain Rs 9 lakh crore! Nifty50 above 24,200; BSE Sensex rallies over 1,200 points
content: Stock market today: Sensex and Nifty50 rallied strongly in trade on Wednesday after optimism surrounding renewed US-Iran peace talks led oil prices to drop. While Nifty50 went above 24,200, BSE Sensex rose over 1,200 points.

title: How a 'long chat' with MS Dhoni helped Noor Ahmad dismantle KKR, CSK coach reveals
content: Noor delivered a match-winning performance with figures of 3/21 in four overs as Chennai Super Kings defeated Kolkata Knight Riders in their IPL 2026 match on Tuesday. He played a key role in breaking the middle order as Kolkata lost control of the chase.

title: No ships got through in first 24 hrs: How US is enforcing a sweeping blockade on Iranian ports
content: US forces have imposed a naval blockade on Iranian ports, halting all maritime trade in and out of the country.  Over 10,000 personnel and numerous warships are enforcing the operation, impacting vessels of all nations.  This move escalates regional tensions, with global energy markets already feeling the strain.

title: Ajinkya Rahane fined Rs 12 lakh after KKR's fourth loss in IPL 2026
content: In the match, Noor Ahmad returned figures of 3/21 as Chennai Super Kings beat Kolkata Knight Riders by 32 runs to register their second win in a row.

title: Yash's Ravana costume revealed; actor confirms 'no scenes' with Ranbir
content: CinemaCon 2026 unveiled striking costumes for 'Ramayana: Part One', featuring Yash's elaborate Ravana armour, Ranbir Kapoor's regal Lord Rama, and Sai Pallavi's simple Sita avatar.  Surprisingly, Yash confirmed he and Kapoor do not share any scenes in the first instalment, focusing on their separate kingdoms. Both actors expressed mutual respect and a shared vision for the ambitious project.

title: Delhi-Dehradun expressway inaugurated, but a house still holds up Loni ramp plan
content: A legal dispute over 1,600 sqm of land in Loni's Mandola is delaying a crucial ramp for the newly inaugurated Delhi-Saharanpur-Dehradun Economic Corridor. The unresolved compensation claim, dating back to 1998, has left a key stretch unfinished, creating an accident-prone area and impacting local connectivity.

title: 4 MP cops sleep on hospital bed, pee in dustbin, suspended
content: Four on-duty policemen in Sidhi, Madhya Pradesh, have been suspended after a video emerged showing them sleeping on a patient's bed and urinating in a hospital dustbin. The officers, guarding an undertrial prisoner, were reportedly heavily intoxicated. An inquiry confirmed their misconduct, raising serious questions about discipline within the force during sensitive duties.

title: Tamil Nadu polls: Vijay’s Tiruppur rally called off after 15 faint in scorching heat
content: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam president Vijay's rally in Tiruppur was cancelled after at least 15 supporters collapsed due to severe heat and overcrowding. The decision was made following police advice to prevent a repeat of the Karur stampede, with Vijay departing for Coimbatore.

title: US Navy destroyer intercepts two oil tankers trying to leave Hormuz; orders them to turn back
content: A US Navy destroyer intercepted two oil tankers leaving Iran, marking the initial phase of a US-declared blockade aimed at pressuring Tehran. Despite the blockade, some Iran-linked vessels reportedly transited the Strait of Hormuz. Experts caution about the blockade's effectiveness and warn of potential escalation and retaliation from Iran.

title: 3 players, one crown: How can India's Vaishali win Women's Candidates at eleventh hour?
content: In the running for the title, there are three contenders: India’s Vaishali Rameshbabu, Kazakhstan's Bibisara Assaubayeva, and China’s Zhu Jiner. Vaishali and Bibisara currently lead with 7.5/13 points, followed by Jiner on 7/13. The math is simple yet somewhat disquieting. If Vaishali and Bibisara both win their final games, they head into a tie-break on Thursday.

title: Happy Vishu 2026: Top 50 wishes, images, messages to celebrate Malayalam new year
content: Kerala celebrates Vishu on April 15, 2026, marking the Malayalam New Year with a focus on renewal and prosperity. The festival emphasizes the significance of the Vishukkani, the first auspicious sights at dawn, believed to shape the year ahead. Families gather to welcome fresh beginnings, fostering hope, abundance, and gratitude for a successful and joyful year.

title: Delhi fire: 3, including toddler, dead as massive blaze tears through Rohini shanties
content: A devastating fire ripped through a Rohini shanty settlement early Wednesday. A couple and their three-year-old daughter perished in the blaze. Firefighters battled the flames for hours. The fire spread rapidly due to plastic waste. Several shanties were destroyed. Investigations into the cause are underway.

title: Higher Trump tariffs soon? US treasury secy Bessent says rates could be restored by July
content: Trump is attempting to rebuild his tariff regime using alternative legal avenues after the court ruled that his earlier reliance on emergency powers to impose duties was unconstitutional.

title: Oil prices today: Crude steadies after slump as US, Iran eye fresh peace talks
content: Oil prices dipped for a second day as hopes for US-Iran talks eased supply worries. Brent crude futures fell to $94.27, and WTI to $90.24, though later saw a slight uptick. Diplomatic signals suggest negotiations might restart, potentially impacting flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: ‘Why did top cop share stage with Ashok Kharat?’: Activist flags Nashik godman link
content: Activist Anjali Damania has demanded a probe into a senior Nashik police officer's presence at an event with accused godman Ashok Kharat, citing 18 linked calls. Meanwhile, a Congress MP fears an "encounter" killing as the investigation into Kharat's sexual assault and cheating cases expands, potentially implicating ruling party members. The Maharashtra minister urged cooperation with the probe.

title: Spo: Ball should've been tossed for Adebayo trip
content: Heat center Bam Adebayo suffered a lower back injury after a fall in the second quarter and was ruled out in the second half.

title: Blazers ride Avdija's 41 over Suns, grab 7th seed
content: Deni Avdija scored 41 points to help the Blazers erase an 11-point deficit in the fourth quarter for a 114-110 win over the Suns that  secured the No. 7 seed in the Western Conference playoffs.

content: The Giants and star defensive tackle Dexter Lawrence II have reached an impasse on talks over a new contract extension, sources told NFL Network on Tuesday night.

title: Switched Planes, Took Bus, Train: Iranian Delegation Claims Threats After Failed US Talks
content: The political analyst said that the Iranian delegation faced direct security threats while heading for negotiations with the United States in Islamabad. "As a result, on the way back to Tehran, the delegation secretly switched planes," he said.

title: "Transformative Step": India's 1st Woman President Praises Women's Quota Bill In Letter To PM Modi
content: Women's Reservation Bill, or Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, provides for one-third reservation for women in legislative bodies. It was passed in Parliament in 2023.

title: Fury is back at heavyweight; where does he land?
content: Tyson Fury ends his short-lived retirement by routing arslanbek Makhmudov . the win allows Fury to return to the rankings at heavyweight after a brief absence . "The Gypsy King" slots in at No. 2 behind the only man to have beaten him in his pro career, Oleksandr Usyk .
